description_long: This object is a delicate flowering plant with slender green stems and five prominent, bell-shaped blossoms. The flowers are a soft lavender color, each with five pointed petals that gently flare outward, creating a star-like appearance from certain angles. The petals are thin and slightly translucent, with subtle veining visible on their surfaces. The stems are thin, flexible, and branch out to support the individual flowers, which are spaced apart in a loose, airy arrangement. There are no visible leaves, and the overall structure is light and graceful, typical of wildflowers found in urban parks or roadside verges. The blossoms are oriented in various directions, giving the plant a natural, organic look.
description: A slender green-stemmed plant with five soft lavender, bell-shaped flowers, each with five pointed petals. The blossoms are spaced apart and arranged in a loose, airy cluster.
description_view_0: This view shows the plant with four blossoms facing outward and one angled upward, highlighting the open, star-like shape of the petals.
description_view_1: From this angle, the flowers are aligned vertically, with two blossoms facing directly forward and their five-pointed petals clearly visible.
description_view_2: This side view reveals the branching stems and the spatial arrangement of the five blossoms, with petals overlapping slightly.
description_view_3: The flowers are stacked vertically, showing the bell-shaped profile and the gentle outward flare of the petals.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial configuration, with five petals per flower overlapping slightly at the base and spreading outward symmetrically, forming a star-like or bell-shaped corolla typical of campanulate flowers.
